to.p: provide accommodation for a child by

ul.li: (a) placing him (subject to subsection (2)) with — (i) a family;
(ii) a relative of his;
or (iii) any other suitable person,
on such terms as to payment by the organisation and otherwise as the organisation may determine (subject to section 49 of the Children Act 2004);

(aa) maintaining him in a children's home in respect of which a person is registered under Part 2 of the Care Standards Act 2000 or Part 1 of the Regulation and Inspection of Social Care (Wales) Act 2016;
(f) making such other arrangements (subject to subsection (3)) as seem appropriate to them

condition.p: the voluntary organisation provide accommodation for the child.

to.p: maintain a child in a home provided, equipped and maintained by an appropriate national authority under section 82(5) on such terms as that national authority may from time to time determine

condition.p: under subsection (1)(aa) the voluntary organisation maintains a child in a home provided, equipped and maintained by an appropriate national authority under section 82(5).

to.p: make regulations as to the placing of children with foster parents by voluntary organisations.
make regulations as to the arrangements which may be made under subsection (1)(f).

for.p: regulations made in relation to England under subsection (2) or (3)

to.p: in particular make provision which (with any necessary modifications) is similar to that which may be made under section 22C by virtue of any of paragraphs 12B, 12E and 12F of Schedule 2.

for.p: regulations made in relation to Wales under subsection (2) or (3)

to.p: in particular make provision which (with any necessary modifications) is similar to that which may be made under sections 81 or 87 of the Social Services and Well-being (Wales) Act 2014, including provision which may be made under section 87 in accordance with the examples given in sections 89, 92 and 93 of that Act.
make regulations requiring any voluntary organisation who are providing accommodation for a child to

ul.li: (a)review his case and (b)to consider any representations (including any complaint) made to them by any person falling within a prescribed class of person,
in accordance with the provisions of the regulations.

for.p: the regulations made in relation to England under subsection (4)

to.p: in particular

ul.li: (a)apply with modifications any provision of section 25A or 25B (b)make provision which (with any necessary modifications) is similar to any provision which may be made under section 25A,
25B or 26

for.p: regulations made in relation to Wales under subsection (4)

to.p: in particular make provision which (with any necessary modifications) is similar to that which may be made under sections 99, 100 or 102 of the Social Services and Well-being (Wales) Act 2014

provide that any person who, without reasonable excuse, contravenes or fails to comply with a regulation shall be guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding level 4 on the standard scale.

ul.li: (a)safeguard and promote a child's welfare,
(b)make use of the services and facilities available for children cared for by their parents as appears to the organisation reasonable in his case and (c)to advise,
assist and befriend him with a view to promoting his welfare when he ceases to be accommodated

condition.p: the child is accommodated by or on behalf of a voluntary organisation.

to.p: so far as is reasonably practicable, ascertain the wishes and feelings of

ul.li: (a)the child,
(b)his parents,
(c)any person who is not a parent of his but who has parental responsibility for him and (d)any other person whose wishes and feelings the organisation consider to be relevant,
regarding the matter to be decided

condition.p: making any decision with respect to any such child.

to.p: give due consideration

ul.li: (a)having regard to the child’s age and understanding,
to such wishes and feelings of his as they have been able to ascertain (b)to such other wishes and feelings mentioned in subsection (2) as they have been able to ascertain;
and (c)to the child’s religious persuasion,
racial origin and cultural and linguistic background

condition.p: making any decisions.

to.p: satisfy themselves that any voluntary organisation providing accommodation—

ul.li: (a) within the authority’s area for any child;
or (b) outside that area for any child on behalf of the authority,
are satisfactorily safeguarding and promoting the welfare of the children so provided with accommodation.

condition.p: this is in relation to any voluntary organisation which is an institution within the further education sector, as defined in section 91 of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992, a 16 to 19 Academy or a school.

to.p: arrange for children who are accommodated within their area by or on behalf of voluntary organisations to be visited occasionally in the interests of their welfare

condition.p: this is in relation to community homes

this is in relation to any voluntary organisation which is an institution within the further education sector, as defined in section 91 of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992, a 16 to 19 Academy or a school.

to.p: make regulations

ul.li: (a)requiring every child who is accommodated within a local authority’s area,
by or on behalf of a voluntary organisation,

to be visited by an officer of the authority (i)in prescribed circumstances and (ii)on specified occasions or within specified periods and (b)imposing requirements which must be met by any local authority,
or officer of a local authority,
carrying out functions under this section

condition.p: this is in relation to any voluntary organisation which is an institution within the further education sector, as defined in section 91 of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992, a 16 to 19 Academy or a school.

ul.li: (a) take such steps as are reasonably practicable to secure that the care and accommodation of a child is undertaken by— (i) a parent of his;
(ii) any person who is not a parent of his but who has parental responsibility for him;
or (iii) a relative of his;
and (b) consider the extent to which (if at all) they should exercise any of their functions with respect to the child.

condition.p: the authority are not satisfied that the welfare of any child who is accommodated by or on behalf of a voluntary organisation is being satisfactorily safeguarded or promoted
they consider that it would not be in the best interests of the child

this is in relation to any voluntary organisation which is an institution within the further education sector, as defined in section 91 of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992, a 16 to 19 Academy or a school.

for.p: Any person authorised by a local authority

to.p: for the purpose of enabling the authority to discharge their duties under this section—

ul.li: (a) enter,
at any reasonable time,
and inspect any premises in which children are being accommodated as mentioned in subsection (1) or (2);
(b) inspect any children there;

(c) require any person to furnish him with such records of a kind required to be kept by regulations made under section 22 of the Care Standards Act 2000 or section 20 of the Health and Social Care Act 2008 ,
or section 27 of the Regulation and Inspection of Social Care (Wales) Act 2016 (anaw 2) (in whatever form they are held),
or allow him to inspect such records,
as he may at any time direct

condition.p: this is in relation to any voluntary organisation which is an institution within the further education sector, as defined in section 91 of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992, a 16 to 19 Academy or a school.

for.p: any person exercising the power conferred by subsection (6)

to.p: produce some duly authenticated document showing his authority to do so

condition.p: asked to do so

this is in relation to any voluntary organisation which is an institution within the further education sector, as defined in section 91 of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992, a 16 to 19 Academy or a school.

for.p: any person authorised to exercise the power to inspect records conferred by subsection (6)

to.p: be entitled at any reasonable time to have access to, and inspect and check the operation of, any computer and any associated apparatus or material which is or has been in use in connection with the records in question

condition.p: this is in relation to any voluntary organisation which is an institution within the further education sector, as defined in section 91 of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992, a 16 to 19 Academy or a school.

for.p: any person authorised to exercise the power to inspect records conferred by subsection (6)

to.p: require— (i) the person by whom or on whose behalf the computer is or has been so used; or (ii) any person having charge of, or otherwise concerned with the operation of, the computer, apparatus or material, to afford him such assistance as he may reasonably require

condition.p: this is in relation to any voluntary organisation which is an institution within the further education sector, as defined in section 91 of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992, a 16 to 19 Academy or a school.

to.p: intentionally obstructs another in the exercise of any power conferred by subsection (6) or (8)

condition.p: this is in relation to any voluntary organisation which is an institution within the further education sector, as defined in section 91 of the Further and Higher Education Act 1992, a 16 to 19 Academy or a school.

for.p: the person carrying on a private children’s home

ul.li: (a)safeguard and promote the child’s welfare (b)make use of the services and facilities available for children cared for by their own parents as appears to that person reasonable in the case of the child (c)advise,
assist and befriend him with a view to promoting his welfare when he ceases to be so accommodated

condition.p: the child is accommodated in the home.

for.p: the person carrying on the home

to.p: so far as is reasonably practicable, ascertain the wishes and feelings of—

ul.li: (a) the child;
(b) his parents;
(c) any other person who is not a parent of his but who has parental responsibility for him;
and (d) any person whose wishes and feelings the person carrying on the home considers to be relevant,
regarding the matter to be decided

condition.p: making any decision with respect to any such child the person carrying on the home.

to.p: give due consideration

ul.li: (a)having regard to the child’s age and understanding,
to such wishes and feelings of his as he has ascertained (b)to such other wishes and feelings mentioned in subsection (2) as he has been able to ascertain and (c)to the child’s religious persuasion,
racial origin and cultural and linguistic background

condition.p: making any such decision

for.p: a person (“P”) who is disqualified (under section 68) from fostering a child privately

to.p: carry on, or be otherwise concerned in the management of, or have any financial interest in, a children's home in England

ul.li: (a)P has,
within the period of 28 days beginning with the day on which P became aware of P's disqualification,
disclosed to the appropriate authority the fact that P is so disqualified,
and (b)P has obtained the appropriate authority's written consent.

to.p: employ a person (“P”) who is so disqualified in a children's home in England

condition.p: a)E has, within the period of 28 days beginning with the day on which E became aware of P's disqualification, disclosed to the appropriate authority the fact that P is so disqualified, and (b)E has obtained the appropriate authority's written consent.

for.p: a person who is disqualified (under section 68) from fostering a child privately

to.p: carry on, or be otherwise concerned in the management of, or have any financial interest in a children’s home in Wales

ul.li: (a)disclosed to the appropriate authority the fact that he is so disqualified;
and (b)obtained its written consent.

to.p: employ a person who is so disqualified in a children’s home in Wales

condition.p: he has

ul.li: (a)disclosed to the appropriate authority the fact that that person is so disqualified and (b)obtained its written consent.

to.p: inform the applicant by a written notice which stating

ul.li: (a)the reason for the refusal (b)the applicant’s right to appeal under section 65A against the refusal to the First-tier Tribunal (c)the time within which he may do so

condition.p: the appropriate authority refuses to give its consent under this section.

to.p: confirm the authority’s decision or direct it to give the consent in question

condition.p: receiving an appeal.

to.p: by regulations make provision as to the circumstances in which a person who provides accommodation to a child is, or is not, to be treated as providing him with accommodation in the person’s own home.

satisfy themselves that the welfare of children who are or are proposed to be privately fostered within their area is being or will be satisfactorily safeguarded and promoted and to secure that such advice is given to those concerned with them as appears to the authority to be needed.
make regulations

ul.li: (a)requiring every child who is privately fostered within a local authority’s area to be visited by an officer of the authority (i)in prescribed circumstances;
and (ii)on specified occasions or within specified periods and (b)imposing requirements which are to be met by any local authority,
or officer of a local authority,
in carrying out functions under this section

to.p: impose requirements as to the action to be taken by a local authority for the purposes of discharging their duty under subsection (1) where they have received notification of a proposal that a child be privately fostered.

for.p: any person who is authorised by a local authority to visit for the purpose has reasonable cause to believe that—

to.p: at any reasonable time inspect those premises and any children there.

for.p: any person exercising the power under subsection (3)

to.p: produce some duly authenticated document showing his authority to do so

condition.p: the person is so required.

ul.li: (a) take such steps as are reasonably practicable to secure that the care and accommodation of the child is undertaken by (i)a parent of his (ii)any person who is not a parent of his but who has parental responsibility for him or (iii)a relative of his and (b)consider the extent to which (if at all) they should exercise any of their functions under this Act with respect to the child

condition.p: the local authority is not satisfied that the welfare of any child who is or is proposed to be privately fostered within their area is being or will be satisfactorily safeguarded or promoted
they consider that it would not be in the best interests of the child.

to.p: make regulations requiring a local authority to monitor the way in which the authority discharge their functions under this Part
in particular require the authority to appoint an officer for that purpose.
foster a child privately if he is disqualified from doing so by regulations made by the Secretary of State for the purposes of this section
in particular, provide for a person to be so disqualified where—

ul.li: (a) an order of a kind specified in the regulations has been made at any time with respect to him;
(b) an order of a kind so specified has been made at any time with respect to any child who has been in his care;
(c) a requirement of a kind so specified has been imposed at any time with respect to any such child,
under or by virtue of any enactment;
(d) he has been convicted of any offence of a kind so specified,
or. . . discharged absolutely or conditionally for any such offence;
(e) a prohibition has been imposed on him at any time under section 69 or under any other specified enactment;
(f) his rights and powers with respect to a child have at any time been vested in a specified authority under a specified enactment.

to.p: foster a child privately

ul.li: (a)he lives in the same household as a person who is himself prevented from fostering a child by subsection (1) or (b)he lives in a household at which any such person is employed

condition.p: he has disclosed the fact to the appropriate local authority and obtained their written consent.

to.p: foster a child privately

ul.li: (a)he is barred from regulated activity relating to children (within the meaning of section 3(2) of the Safeguarding Vulnerable Groups Act 2006) or (b)he lives in the same household as a person who is barred from such activity.

to.p: inform the applicant by a written notice which states

ul.li: (a)the reason for the refusal (b)the applicant’s right under paragraph 8 of Schedule 8 to appeal against the refusal and (c)the time within which he may do so

condition.p: the authority refuses to give their consent under this section.

to.p: impose a prohibition on a person privately fostering under subsection (3)

condition.p: the local authority for the area within which the child is proposed to be, or is being, fostered are of the opinion that

ul.li: (a)he is not a suitable person to foster a child (b)the premises in which the child will be,
or is being,
accommodated are not suitable or (c)it would be prejudicial to the welfare of the child for him to be,
or continue to be accommodated by that person in those premises

for.p: A prohibition imposed on any person under this subsection

to.p: prohibit him from fostering privately

ul.li: (a)any child in any premises within the area of the local authority or (b)any child in premises specified in the prohibition (c)a child identified in the prohibition,
in premises specified in the prohibition

for.p: a local authority who have imposed a prohibition on any person under subsection (3)

to.p: cancel the prohibition

ul.li: (a)of their own motion or (b)on an application made by that person

condition.p: they think fit
they are satisfied that the prohibition is no longer justified.

to.p: impose a prohibition on him under subsection (3)

condition.p: the local authority imposes a requirement on any person under paragraph 6 of Schedule 8

for.p: any prohibition imposed by virtue of subsection (5)

to.p: have effect

ul.li: (a)the time specified for compliance with the requirement has expired and (b)the requirement has not been compiled with

for.p: a prohibition imposed under this section

to.p: be imposed by notice in writing addressed to the person on whom it is imposed and informing him of

ul.li: (a)the reason for imposing the prohibition (b)his right under paragraph 8 of Schedule 8 to appeal against the prohibition and (c)the time within which he may do so
(a) being required,
under any provision made by or under this Part,
give any notice or information— (i) he fails without reasonable excuse to give the notice within the time specified in that provision;
or (ii) he fails without reasonable excuse to give the information within a reasonable time;
or (iii) he makes,
or causes or procures another person to make,
any statement in the notice or information which he knows to be false or misleading in a material particular;
(b) refuse to allow a privately fostered child to be visited by a duly authorised officer of a local authority;
(c) intentionally obstruct another in the exercise of the power conferred by section 67(3);
(d) contravene section 68;
(e) fail without reasonable excuse to comply with any requirement imposed by a local authority under this Part;
(f) accommodate a privately fostered child in any premises in contravention of a prohibition imposed by a local authority under this Part;
(g) knowingly cause to be published,
or publishes,
an advertisement which he knows contravenes paragraph 10 of Schedule 8.

to.p: be brought at any time within six months from the date when evidence of the offence came to the knowledge of the local authority.

condition.p: any person who is required, under any provision of this Part, to give a notice fails to give the notice within the time specified in that provision.
